problem_title: Travel Rule messaging disrupts case workflows
problem_description: Failed or incomplete IVMS101 messages create gaps in Travel Rule compliance processes for VASPs. These disruptions slow case reviews, fragment audit evidence and increase manual follow-up efforts across compliance teams.
problem_point_1_value: KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule outputs sit in separate tools
problem_point_2_value: Teams move data between dashboards, spreadsheets and internal notes
problem_point_3_value: Regulatory changes are hard to map into internal workflows
problem_point_4_value: Evidence and decision history are difficult to reconstruct later
solution_description: SFTY routes failed or incomplete Travel Rule messages into structured cases while linking originator and beneficiary data, workflow status, provider outputs and audit evidence for streamlined compliance review.
workflow_title: From failed Travel Rule messages to cases
workflow_description: SFTY captures failed IVMS101 messages and routes exceptions through case creation, reviewer assessment, evidence collection and compliance reporting workflows.
workflow_step_1_description: KYC, KYB, KYT and screening outputs enter SFTY
workflow_step_2_description: Risk rules route checks into the right workflow
workflow_step_3_description: A case opens with customer, wallet and provider context
workflow_step_4_description: Teams review, decide and record the outcome
workflow_step_5_description: Logs, decisions and reports stay ready for audit

why_sfty_card_3_description: Preserve evidence, reviewer actions and decision history
why_sfty_card_4_description: Give compliance, risk and leadership one operating view

ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for product, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on provider orchestration, Travel Rule workflows, audit evidence and enterprise compliance operations.
faq_1_question: How does SFTY handle failed IVMS101 messages?
faq_1_answer: SFTY routes failed IVMS101 messages into structured compliance workflows, triggering review, exception handling, case creation and evidence collection for clearer operational oversight.
faq_2_question: How are failed Travel Rule cases managed?
faq_2_answer: Failed Travel Rule messages are linked to compliance cases with assigned reviewers, decision logs and escalation paths to support timely resolution and audit readiness.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve Travel Rule evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves message data, decision logs and case evidence to maintain a full audit trail for Travel Rule exceptions and compliance reviews.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY connect existing Travel Rule providers?
faq_4_answer: Yes. SFTY integrates multiple Travel Rule providers, consolidating their message outputs and statuses into one connected compliance workflow.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace Travel Rule message providers?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY does not replace Travel Rule providers but connects their outputs into structured workflows and case management for operational efficiency.
final_title: Route failed Travel Rule messages into workflows
final_description: Review how SFTY connects failed IVMS101 messages, originator data, exceptions and cases into audit-ready compliance operations.
